content.full_text:
PAUL H. CHAMBERLAIN
RUSSELL P. BALDO
C ga.r"rsenLArN, Crr-e,r"rnnnr-ArN & Be.roo
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
BANK OF CA,LIFORNIA BUILDING
P. O. BOX 32
AIIBURN, CAIIFOBNIA 95604.OO32
(9r6) Aas-4523
FAx: (9rG) aas-5ar4
L. L. CHAM BERLAIN, IAAA.I9I3
T. L. CHAMBERLAIN, I9I3.I975
T, L. CHAMBERLAIN, JR,, I95O.I984
August L6, 1991
Fair Political- Practices
Commission - Legal Department
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814
Attn: 
Blanca Breeze
Dear it'ls. Breeze:
As general counsel for the Tahoe City Public Utility
District, I request a formal advice letter 
( "A" ) on a
matter involving a potential confli-ct of interest.
BACKGROUND DATA
Tahoe City PubIic Utility District and the North
Tahoe Public Utility District are each utility 
districts
duly organized under California 1aw, Public Utilities 
Code
Sections 15500 and following. 
The geographicaf territory
of Tahoe City Public Utility District extends generally
from Emeral-d Bay on the West Shore of Lake Tahoe, Northerly
along the !'Iest Shore terminating at the summit of Do}lar
Hill which lies Northerly of the unincorporated town known
as Tahoe City. 
North Tahoe Public Utility District extends
generally from the DoIIar Hill area along the West and
North Shores of Lake Tahoe ending at the California/Nevada
State Line.
Both utility 
districts provide water and sewer
services within their respective territories 
and each
engage in and provide recreational services, activities 
and
various facilities 
as we1I.
fn recent years the boards of directors of the
respective districts in conjunction wj-th a representative
on the Pl-acer County Board of Supervisors representing the
North Lake Tahoe Area have expressed interest and a desire
to consider undertaking initial 
studies to determine the
feasibility 
and consider initial 
planning stages for
regional reereation and community facilities 
projects of
benefit to the respective areas, their respective
inhabitants ano visitors.


August 16, l9 91
Page Two
FACTS
In April of 1991 Tahoe City Public Utility Distrj-ct
was designated as the Iead agency for the cooperative
planning processes, including control and direction.
As an initial 
measure, there was formed a "Joint
Regional Recreation Conunittee" comprised of representatives
from each of the utility 
districts, 
as we.If as
representation on behalf of Pfacer County through the
County Executive's Office (since the County is concerned in
the facilities, 
scope of study as well as financial
matters ) and there is also representation from the "Tahoe
Transient Occupancy Tax Advisory Committee" (an advisory
agency to the Board of Supervisors since a portion of the
anticipated funding comes from the Transient Occupancy
Taxes generated in the Tahoe area of Placer County).
There is no formal hrr.itten agreement between the two
districts and Pfacer County.
There is a "steering corunittee" comprised of
representatives of the tvTo utility 
districts 
and Placer
County from the "Joint Regional Recreation Committee" which
"steering committee" acts as an ad hoc advisory committee
to Tahoe City Public Utility Dlstrict, as lead agency.
The initial 
planning processes involving the
Regional Recreational Master Plan and Conununity Facilities
Pl-an was the obtaining, preparation and transmittal of
request for proposals for design and architectural services
as well as separate proposals for "public participation or
involvement. Tahoe City Public Utility District as the
lead agency prepareo and processed 'uhe requests for
proposals and numerous proposals were received. These
responses were then screened by a committee which graded
the proponents and made recommendations for selectj-on back
to the board of directors of Tahoe City Public Uti}ity
District who then took action approving contracting with
Sagan Design for the architecturaf/des ign services, and
reconrnending contracting with wild west Graphics and
Communications to provide the public participation services
as public involvement coordinator.


August 16, I9 91
Page Three
The current conflict of interest question involves
the sefection of Wild west Graphics to undertake and
provide the public participation services where principals
of wild West craphics are also financially involved with
the North Tahoe Fine Arts Councif.
The services to be provided by WiId West craphics
and Communications as the publ j-c involvement coordinator
are outlj-ned in Exhibit uA' to the draft forrn of Agreement
between Tahoe City Public Utj,lity District and WiId West
Graphics, which draft agreement, including exhibit thereto
of Exhibit "A" is attached to this letter, marked Exhibit
"I". 
In addition there is attached the letter statement of
facts to the undersigned, pursuant to which l.egal opinion
was being requested which fetter was dated July 19, 1991.
As the contract and exhibit attached indicate, one
of the areas of consideration and study may be involved
with the performing arts or artj-stic endeavors.
Essentially the problem arises, therefore, by virtue of the
facts that Ed l4i11er and Lo1ly Kupec, who are principals in
Wifd West Graphics are also invofved in providing services
to the North Tahoe Fine Arts Council, a non-profit
corporation servj-ng the North Lake Tahoe areas of Placer
and Nevada Counties as well as a port j-on in the State of
Nevada, which involves generally the improvement of Ievels
of art consciousness and providing of arts in the North
Lake Tahoe area. Ed Mj.I1er is a contract employee acting
as chief executive of the North Tahoe Fine Arts Councif,
Vo.Iunteer Chairman, and LolIy Kupec is a Volunteer
President Emeritus of the Board of Directors of North Tahoe
Fine Arts Council. Ed Mil]er ls also a managing partner of
Wild West Graphics and Lolly Kupec is creative director of
Wild West Graphics.
The "scope of work" submj-tted and to be performed by
Wild West Graphics is outlined j-n Exhibit 
the draft
form of Agreement for Professional Services attached hereto
marked Exhibit "1".
Wifd West Graphics and the principals wiII not have
authority to make decisions for the contracting agency,


August 16, 19 9I
Page Four
Tahoe City Pubfic Utility District, or for the Joint
Regional Recreation Committee.
QUESTION: Is Wild West Graphics or the principals, Ivlilfer
and/or Kupec, in provj-ding public participation services as
public endowment coordinator, acting as a consuftant to
Tahoe City Pubfic Utility District or the Joint Regionaf
Committee and, if so, are these principals, Ed l4ilfer
and/ or Lolly KupecT acting as public officers in a position
of conflict by virtue of their also being volunteer
officers and/or receiving compensation for services for
other activitres invofved in the North Tahoe Eine Arts
Counc i 1 ?
If principals of Wifd west Graphics are acting as
consuliants, or either of them, would they stiff be abfe to
provide public participation services in areas of
consideration or interests in activitj-es, programs or
other than those refated to arts, artistic 
endeavors,
perTorffins g1qg$ff*Z
plans
or
Time is important in this matter in that the program
and the services and activities anticipated are
contemplated to become active and move forward starting in
earfy September and hence early review, corunent and advice
would be most appreciated.
If there is any additional information,
documentation or data needed, please feel free to let us
know and we will be glad to provide it as soon as possible.
Thanking you in advance for your courtesy and help, we
remain
Respectfully yours,
CHATiB
CHAMBERLAIN & BALDO
By:
PHC Kt
Russell P. Bal-do
